title: 📝 Training ⭐⭐⭐: safe and fit — full review

questions.prompt: Complete: "How ___ water do you drink every day?"
questions.explanation: "Water" is a thing you cannot count one by one, so it takes "much": How much water do you drink? "Many" goes with countable things like eggs or apples, and "some"/"any" do not fit the "How ___" question about quantity.

questions.prompt: Complete: "The trees are moving and my hat flew away. It is very ___ today."
questions.explanation: After "is very" we need the adjective, built with Noun + y: wind → windy. "Wind" is the noun, "windly" wrongly adds -ly (that makes adverbs), and "winded" is not a weather adjective.

questions.prompt: It is snowing outside. Choose the correct suggestion.
questions.explanation: "Let's" is followed by the base verb: Let's make a snowman. "Let's makes" adds a third-person -s, "Let's making" adds -ing, and "Let's to make" adds an extra "to" — none of these follow "Let's".

questions.prompt: A pupil is running in the corridor. What does the teacher say?
questions.explanation: After "Don't" we use the base verb with nothing added: Don't run! "Don't runs" keeps an -s, "Don't running" adds -ing, and "Don't to run" adds "to" — all wrong after "Don't".

questions.prompt: Complete: "Last weekend, we ___ to the countryside for a picnic."
questions.explanation: "Last weekend" is a finished time, so we need the simple past. "Go" is irregular: its past is "went", not "goed" ✓. The common trap is adding -ed to make "goed" — irregular verbs change their whole form, so it is went.

questions.prompt: The coach gives advice. Choose the correct sentence.

options.text: Eating a balanced diet and not eat too much sugar.
To eat a balanced diet and don't eat too much sugar.
Eat a balanced diet and not eat too much sugar.
Eat a balanced diet and don't eat too much sugar.

questions.explanation: Advice uses the imperative: the base verb for a positive order (Eat a balanced diet) and "don't" + base verb for a negative order (don't eat too much sugar) ✓. The common trap is "not eat" — the negative imperative needs "don't", not just "not"; "Eating" and "To eat" are not imperative forms either.
